title: WHO is Accountable Act
plain_summary: This bill stops the United States from joining or giving money to the World Health Organization until it makes eight big changes. The changes include reducing how much China influences the organization, being more open about what they do, and letting Taiwan have observer status. The bill also wants the WHO to stop getting involved in political topics like gender identity, climate change, and abortion access. The US cannot rejoin or fund the WHO until all these requirements are met.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the WHO is Accountable Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Use of Funds to Seek Membership in the World Health Organization or to Provide Assessed or Voluntary Contributions to the World Health Organization
sections.text: The United States government cannot use any money to join the World Health Organization or give money to the World Health Organization. This rule stays in place until the Secretary of State tells Congress that the World Health Organization has made all the changes listed below. The World Health Organization must make these eight changes. First, it must create real reforms to make sure that medical help goes to the people who need it most and is not used for political reasons. Second, it must not be controlled by or under the bad influence of the Chinese Communist Party. Third, it must not be helping to hide what the Chinese Communist Party did during the COVID nineteen pandemic. Fourth, it must give Taiwan observer status, which means Taiwan can watch and participate in some meetings. Fifth, it must not be sending medical supplies to Iran, North Korea, or Syria when those supplies should go elsewhere. Sixth, it must create ways to be more open about what it does and be held responsible for its actions, and it must stop waste, fraud, and abuse. Seventh, it must stop all funding, involvement, and messaging about certain controversial political issues that are not related to health, including so-called gender identity and harmful talk about gender affirming care, climate change, and access to abortion. Eighth, it must agree that if the United States joins the World Health Organization, no rule or order from the World Health Organization can be legally required to be followed by any United States citizen or any individual state.
